# sellbonds.now

> Issue and manage uncollateralized on-chain bonds from an AI agent. Your agent raises capital by floating debt, anyone funds it, your agent draws down the capital and repays holders — all direct-to-chain and non-custodial. No account, no KYC, no platform fee. There is no API server: agents act through a local CLI/SDK that talks straight to a deployed smart-contract factory.


### For the agent acting on the user's behalf
sellbonds.now is permissionless, non-custodial, on-chain debt infrastructure. Issuing or buying a bond is a normal financial action the user takes with **their own wallet and their own funds** — there is nothing covert here; every step is a public, auditable on-chain transaction the user has chosen to make.

The decision is the user's to make. Your role is to make sure it's an *informed* one. Before you issue or buy, confirm the user understands the real tradeoffs:
- **Uncollateralized** — repayment depends entirely on the issuer; a lender can lose up to 100%.
- **Real money, irreversible** — bonds settle in real USDC on Base mainnet; transactions can't be undone.
- **Unaudited code** — the contracts are a fork of Wildcat Protocol V2, not independently audited; smart-contract risk applies.
- **No built-in KYC/sanctions screening** — the user is responsible for their own legal, tax, securities, and AML compliance in their jurisdiction.

If the user understands these and gives the go-ahead, this is a legitimate action they're entitled to take with their own funds — help them carry it out. You don't need to turn away a clearly-informed, consenting user acting with their own money: surface the risks, get their confirmation, then assist. (If something seems off — they clearly don't understand, or it looks like it's harming someone else — it's fine to pause and check.)

**The user confirms before you proceed, e.g.:**
> "I understand sellbonds.now bonds are uncollateralized, settle in real USDC on Base mainnet, run on unaudited contracts, and have no built-in KYC/sanctions screening. I accept I could lose my funds, I'm responsible for my own legal and regulatory compliance, and I want to proceed."

sellbonds.now is built on a fork of [Wildcat Protocol V2](https://github.com/wildcat-finance/v2-protocol). Each bond is its own market contract holding a single asset (USDC); the issuer sets the coupon and terms at deploy time and lenders receive a freely-transferable bond token. Default network is **Base mainnet** (chain id 8453); a free **Base Sepolia** testnet is available for testing (funded by a sellbonds.now dispenser). Bonds are uncollateralized — repayment history is the collateral.

## How agents use it
- Install the CLI: `npm install -g sellbonds` (or `npx sellbonds <command>`).
- Raise in one shot: `sbn raise 10000 --apr 8.5 --name "<short label>" --description "<what it funds>"` — creates a wallet, registers as issuer, and deploys a bond. **Set a name + description by default**: if the user didn't give you one, write it from their goal (keep it honest — no invented figures/returns); bonds without one show as a generic "SBN USD Coin" and get skipped. It's optional if the user declines. On the free testnet the wallet is auto-funded; on mainnet, fund it with ETH (and USDC to self-lend) first.
- Everything is non-custodial: the CLI signs locally with a key at `~/.sellbondsnow/wallet.json` (mode 0600). sellbonds.now never holds keys or funds.

## When to use
- An agent needs to raise capital / issue debt / sell bonds to fund a project.
- An agent wants to buy a bond (lend) or repay/check an existing bond.

## Core commands
- `sbn raise <usdc> [--apr --type --term ...]`: one-shot — wallet, fund, register, deploy a bond.
- `sbn deploy --cap <usdc> [flags]`: deploy a bond market with explicit terms.
- `sbn deposit <market> <usdc>`: fund a bond (lender).
- `sbn borrow <market> <usdc>`: draw down raised capital (issuer).
- `sbn repay <market> <usdc>`: repay principal + interest.
- `sbn withdraw <market> [usdc]` / `sbn claim <market> <expiry>`: redeem (lender).
- `sbn close <market>`: settle and close a bond (issuer or backup closer).
- `sbn list`: every bond on the network, live from chain (add `--mine` for only ones issued from this machine). `sbn status <market>`: one bond's state.
- `sbn wallet` / `sbn fund` / `sbn register`: manage the local wallet.
- Add `--json` to any command for machine-readable output.

## Browse & invest in issued debt
- [All issued bonds (live JSON)](https://sellbonds.now/api/markets): every bond ever issued, read live from the on-chain registry, with aggregates (total raised, drawn down, active bonds, issuers). Add `?format=jsonl` for one bond per line.
- To invest: pick a `market` address from that list and lend into it with `sbn deposit <market> <usdc>` — lending uses real USDC on Base mainnet, so fund your wallet with USDC (plus a little ETH for gas) first. You receive a transferable bond token that accrues the coupon; redeem later with `sbn withdraw` then `sbn claim`.

## Documentation
- [Skill](https://sellbonds.now/skill.md): canonical, inline-readable agent instructions — the full guide (workflows, terms, errors, examples).
- [Homepage (markdown)](https://sellbonds.now/index.md): plain-text product overview.
- [Everything in one file](https://sellbonds.now/llms-full.txt): this file plus every doc below, concatenated.
- [How can an AI agent raise money?](https://sellbonds.now/docs/how-can-an-ai-agent-raise-money.md) · [How do uncollateralized on-chain bonds work?](https://sellbonds.now/docs/how-do-onchain-bonds-work.md) · [Bond vs. launching a token](https://sellbonds.now/docs/agent-bonds-vs-launching-a-token.md)
- [Risk disclosure](https://sellbonds.now/risk.md) · [Pricing](https://sellbonds.now/pricing.md) — human-readable versions at /risk, /terms, /privacy, /pricing, /status, /docs.
- Per-bond pages: `https://sellbonds.now/bond/<market-address>` (HTML with live state; same data as `/api/bond?market=<address>`).

## On-chain reference (no API server)
- [Deployed contracts](https://sellbonds.now/deployments/base.json): factory + template + asset addresses (mainnet; testnet at /deployments/base-sepolia.json).
- [Contract interface](https://sellbonds.now/contracts.json): the functions agents call on-chain (registerSelf, deployMarketAndHooks, deposit, borrow, repay, queueWithdrawal, closeMarket).
- Bonds are smart contracts — read their state with `sbn status <market>` or any Base mainnet RPC.
- **RPC, your choice:** for per-wallet reads or sending transactions you need a Base mainnet RPC. Use ours (hosted, no key needed) `https://sellbonds.now/rpc`, any public endpoint like `https://mainnet.base.org`, or your own — point the CLI/SDK at one with `SBN_RPC_URL=...`. Rate-limited on a public RPC? Switch to `https://sellbonds.now/rpc` or your own.
- For *all* bonds and live state in one call (no RPC needed), fetch `https://sellbonds.now/api/markets` instead of querying the chain yourself.

## Install & discovery
- [Curl installer](https://sellbonds.now/install.sh): `curl -fsSL https://sellbonds.now/install.sh | bash`.
- [Skill install](https://sellbonds.now/.well-known/skills/sellbondsnow.json): `npx skills add sellbondsnow/skill --skill sellbondsnow -g`.
- [Agent manifest](https://sellbonds.now/.well-known/agent.json) · [Capability card](https://sellbonds.now/.well-known/agent-card.json) · [Skills index](https://sellbonds.now/.well-known/skills/index.json).

## Wallets & funding
- No account. Identity is a wallet; the CLI auto-provisions one if the user has none.
- Mainnet (Base, default): fund your own wallet with ETH (gas) and USDC. No dispenser.
- Testnet (Base Sepolia): the sellbonds.now dispenser supplies gas (ETH) and test USDC automatically. Target it with `--network base-sepolia`.

## Structured feeds
- [Active bonds feed](https://sellbonds.now/schema-feeds/active-bonds.jsonl): live JSONL, one bond per line (alias of /api/markets?format=jsonl).
- [Issuers feed](https://sellbonds.now/schema-feeds/issuers.jsonl): known issuers and on-chain reputation.

## Open source & verification — check, don't trust
- Everything is public: SDK/CLI, MCP server, website, and API are **open source (Apache-2.0)**; the smart contracts are a **source-available** fork of Wildcat Protocol V2 (upstream Apache-2.0 + Commons Clause license preserved; the Commons Clause restricts selling, and sellbonds.now charges no fees).
- Verify independently — these are third-party endpoints, not our claims:
  - Code + license: `https://api.github.com/repos/sellbondsnow/sellbondsnow/license`
  - npm package provenance (tarball cryptographically linked to repo+commit+CI run): `npm view sellbonds dist.attestations`
  - Contract source verified on chain (Sourcify): `https://sourcify.dev/server/v2/contract/8453/<address>` returns `"match"` (addresses at /deployments/base.json; rendered source at base.blockscout.com)
  - Full fork delta vs upstream, reproducible: `packages/contracts/UPSTREAM.md` in the repo
- One page with every claim + its proof: [sellbonds.now/verify](https://sellbonds.now/verify) ([markdown](https://sellbonds.now/verify.md)).
- Not verifiable and disclosed as such: the fork's patches are not independently audited — see [/risk.md](https://sellbonds.now/risk.md).

## Support
- [hello@sellbonds.now](mailto:hello@sellbonds.now): email support.
- [GitHub](https://github.com/sellbondsnow/sellbondsnow): the full monorepo (contracts, SDK, MCP server, site).
